summary: Pure copy fix on the login button label. No behavior change, no auth change — just a visible text string. Surface is UI only and reversibility is trivial (revert the string).

reversibility.why: A string edit is a single-line revert with no schema or migration exposure.

blast_radius.why: One label on one button; no callers, no APIs, no data paths affected.

compliance_scope.why: No regulated data and no identity/consent surface touched — the word 'login' is incidental.

user_facing_impact.why: The label is literally read by every user on the login screen, even if the change is tiny.

novelty.why: Copy fixes are routine; no priors suggest hidden complexity on this surface.

state_complexity.why: No persistent state, no schema, no cache.

operational_risk.why: Rendering a corrected string has no runtime failure modes.

coordination_cost.why: A single frontend hand is sufficient; no cross-team sign-off needed.

specialists.name: frontend-engineer
specialists.why: Owns the component that renders the login button label.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Fix the string inline — no clarify or design needed for a typo.

rigor_why: All risk factors are LOW or trivial; advisory review is enough. A full gate ceremony would be more risky than the change itself.
complexity_why: A single-word string edit sits at the floor of the 0–7 scale.

tasks.title: Fix typo in login button label
